title: LokiRequestLatency alert title mangled
suggested_labels: Type: Bug; Priority: High; good first issue
summary: The LokiRequestLatency alert renders 'Loki request latency (instance ) (LokiRequestLatency)' with an empty instance. The rule's summary references {{ $labels.instance }} but its PromQL expr aggregates with `sum(...) by (le)`, which discards every label except `le` (including instance, job, route). So $labels.instance (and $labels.job/$labels.route used in the description) are empty when the alert fires.
why_good_first_issue: Single rule file, root cause provable by reading the rule, and a small contained fix (either drop the {{ $labels.instance }} from the summary or preserve the needed labels in the `by (...)` grouping). No charm code or live cluster needed to understand it.
repro_result: Confirmed by inspection: line 6 expr = `(histogram_quantile(0.99, sum(rate(loki_request_duration_seconds_bucket{route!~"(?i).*tail.*"}[5m])) by (le))) > 1` -- aggregation is `by (le)` only. Line 11 summary = `Loki request latency (instance {{ $labels.instance }})`. Because `by (le)` drops the instance label, $labels.instance is empty -> exactly the reported 'Loki request latency (instance ) (LokiRequestLatency)'. Same dropped-label problem affects $labels.job/$labels.route in the description (line 12). Observed-vs-expected: title shows empty instance vs should show the firing instance.
fix_hint: Either (a) remove the (instance {{ $labels.instance }}) fragment from the summary (and the job/route refs from the description) since the aggregated series has no such labels, or (b) preserve the labels by widening the grouping, e.g. `... by (le, instance)` / juju topology labels. Note the same `(instance {{ $labels.instance }})` summary pattern also appears in loki_request_errors.rule:11 and loki_request_panic.rule:11 whose exprs group by (namespace, job, route) and (namespace, job) respectively -- neither keeps `instance` -- so consider fixing all three consistently.
notes: Type: Bug + Priority: High in tracker, still open and reproducible from the rule definition. instance label absent because Juju-topology series do not carry a raw `instance` label and the expr aggregates it away regardless.

title: Refactor `LokiOperatorCharm._check_alert_rules`
suggested_labels: Type: Enhancement; Type: Cleanup; good first issue
summary: _check_alert_rules (src/charm.py:959) is misnamed and does too much: the name implies a file/disk check but it actually verifies rules via the Loki HTTP API; it side-effects by writing self._stored.status['rules'] (BlockedStatus/ActiveStatus) instead of letting the caller manage status; and it returns None rather than a bool. The issue asks for a rename, removal of the status side-effect, and a bool return (True on 200, False otherwise).
why_good_first_issue: Well-scoped single-method refactor with an explicit 3-point definition of done in the issue. Touches one method plus its two call sites (charm.py:679 and :867). No new behaviour, easy to cover with existing unit tests.
affected_files: src/charm.py:959-997 (_check_alert_rules); call sites src/charm.py:679 and src/charm.py:867
repro_steps: multipass exec cos -- bash -lc 'cd ~/work/loki-k8s-operator && sed -n "959,997p" src/charm.py' ; and grep -n "_check_alert_rules" src/charm.py for call sites
repro_result: Confirmed by inspection: method def at src/charm.py:959 `def _check_alert_rules(self):` with docstring 'Check alert rules using Loki API.' (so the name '_check_alert_rules' does not convey it's an API call). In every branch it assigns self._stored.status['rules'] = to_tuple(BlockedStatus(...)) or to_tuple(ActiveStatus()) (lines ~985, ~989, ~992, ~997) and `return`s None -- never returns a bool. Matches all three issue points (name, side-effect, missing bool return). Called at charm.py:679 and :867 ignoring any return value.
fix_hint: Rename to something like `_verify_alert_rules_via_api`/`_alert_rules_ok`; have it return True when urlopen succeeds (HTTP 200) and False on HTTPError/URLError/other; move the self._stored.status['rules'] = ... assignments into the callers (charm.py:679, :867) based on the returned bool. Update unit tests accordingly.
notes: Type: Enhancement in tracker but is really a Type: Cleanup refactor; behaviour-preserving. There is an existing comment at charm.py:671-673 already noting the awkward status side-effect, reinforcing the refactor's intent.
